issue_title: Inconsistency in WCS transformations between FITS WCS and GWCS (pixel CS origin)
issue_body:
There is something weird going on related to the "origin" of the coordinate system used for the "image"/pixel coordinates. Specifically, my understanding is that pixels are counted using numpy convention, that is first pixel in a 2D array has "coordinates" (0,0) (unlike (1,1) in DS9). However, my computations show that transformations performed by GWCS correspond to computations performed by analogous functions in FITS WCS *when origin is set to 1*:

```python
>>> from astropy import wcs as fitswcs
>>> fits_wcs = fitswcs.WCS()
>>> fits_wcs.wcs.pc = [[1,0], [0,1]]
>>> fits_wcs.wcs.cdelt = [1e-5, 1e-5]
>>> fits_wcs.wcs.crpix = [100, 100]
>>> fits_wcs.wcs.crval = [250, 2]
>>> fits_wcs.wcs.ctype=['RA---TAN','DEC--TAN']
>>> fits_wcs.wcs.set()
>>> 
>>> import gwcs
>>> from astropy.modeling.models import ( Shift, Scale, RotateNative2Celestial, AffineTransformation2D, Rotation2D, Pix2Sky_TAN )
>>> from gwcs import coordinate_frames
>>> from astropy import coordinates as coord
>>> transform = Shift(-100) & Shift(-100) | Rotation2D(0) | Scale(1e-5) & Scale(1e-5) | Pix2Sky_TAN() | RotateNative2Celestial(250, 2, 180)
>>> sky_frame = coordinate_frames.CelestialFrame(reference_frame=coord.ICRS(), name='icrs')
>>> w = gwcs.WCS(forward_transform=transform, output_frame=sky_frame)
>>> 
>>> w(150,50)
(250.0005003046197, 1.9994999999238419)
>>> fits_wcs.wcs_pix2world([[150,50]], 0)
array([[ 250.00051031,    1.99951   ]])
>>> fits_wcs.wcs_pix2world([[150,50]], 1)
array([[ 250.0005003,    1.9995   ]])
>>> 
>>> # it may be easier to see the differences:
>>> wp = w(150,50)
>>> fits_wcs.wcs_pix2world([[150,50]], 0) - wp
array([[  1.00060955e-05,   9.99999694e-06]])
>>> fits_wcs.wcs_pix2world([[150,50]], 1) - wp
array([[ -2.84217094e-14,   6.43929354e-15]])
```

Am I doing something wrong?

issue_title: Report callback
issue_body:
The C library has a punch of callbacks and support code that was only used by `autohintexe`, namely the options:
```
   -ra Write alignment zones data. Does not hint or change glyph. Default extension is '.rpt'
   -rs Write stem widths data. Does not hint or change glyph. Default extension is '.rpt'
   -a Modifies -ra and -rs: Includes stems between curved lines: default is to omit these.
```

The `autohint` Python script (and consequently `psautohint`) does not expose any of these options. Any idea if they are still in use and how to test them?

issue_title: [Documentation Issue] Pub/Sub default max_messages is unclear in 0.13.0 docs
issue_body:
Page Name: pubsub-usage
Release: 0.13.0

This section: 

> Fetch pending messages for a pull subscription:

```
>>> from gcloud import pubsub
>>> client = pubsub.Client()
>>> topic = client.topic('topic_name')
>>> subscription = topic.subscription('subscription_name')
>>> with topic.batch() as batch:
...     batch.publish('this is the first message_payload')
...     batch.publish('this is the second message_payload',
...                   attr1='value1', attr2='value2')
>>> received = subscription.pull()  # API request
>>> messages = [recv[1] for recv in received]
>>> [message.message_id for message in messages]
[<message_id1>, <message_id2>]
>>> [message.data for message in messages]
['this is the first message_payload', 'this is the second message_payload']
>>> [message.attributes for message in messages]
[{}, {'attr1': 'value1', 'attr2': 'value2'}]
```

Does not pull all pending messages in my very simple test code.  It only pulls one pending message.  I can get multiple by specifying 'max_messages' greater than 1 in the 'subscription.pull()' call.


Has something changed in the implementation since this doc was written?

issue_title: Spanner: generator raises StopIteration under Python 3.7 (due to PEP 479)
issue_body:
Running the following code (modified slightly from actual example, for clarity) under Python 3.7 results in RuntimeError('generator raised StopIteration').

~~~~~~
from google.cloud import spanner
import traceback

spanner_client = spanner.Client()
instance = spanner_client.instance('my-test-instance')
database = instance.database('spanner')

with database.snapshot() as snapshot:
  results = snapshot.execute_sql(
      "SELECT 'Success from Spanner!' as message")
  for row in results:
    print(row)

database.close()
~~~~~~

Here's the traceback:

~~~~~~
RuntimeError('generator raised StopIteration')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/google/cloud/spanner_v1/streamed.py", line 140, in __iter__
    self._consume_next()  # raises StopIteration
  File "/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/google/cloud/spanner_v1/streamed.py", line 114, in _consume_next
    response = six.next(self._response_iterator)
StopIteration

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "xxx", line xx, in page_index
    for row in results:
RuntimeError: generator raised StopIteration
~~~~~~

This is the result of [PEP 479](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0479/), which changes the behaviour of StopIteration exceptions raised in generators: pre-3.7, these cause the generator to exit, but in 3.7 they result in a RuntimeError, so must be caught inside the generator.

issue_title: pex pulls in pre-release versions of libraries
issue_body: I recently had an issue where my production build stopped working because it picked up a pre-release version of protobuf. `pex protobuf` will pick up version `3.2.0rc2` right now, while a pip install will pick up `3.1.0.post1`. I didn't expect this to happen and there doesn't appear to be a way to change the behavior.

issue_title: Fix developer thrash on the `native_engine_version` file
issue_body: Right now, we deploy the native engine code as a seperate binary and version the binary via a hash of its sources, the value being stored in `src/python/pants/engine/native_engine_version`. With multiple developers starting to hack on the native engine more regularly, this files contents conflict and the dev experience is suboptimal.

issue_title: Ability to download whole Directory from remote CAS
issue_body:
The [Store](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/628c83d5ab2706b0f64d69568c57a718ec7c5e2a/src/rust/engine/fs/src/store.rs#L40) currently has [a function](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/628c83d5ab2706b0f64d69568c57a718ec7c5e2a/src/rust/engine/fs/src/store.rs#L225-L295) to copy a list of `Digest`s from the local store to the remote one.

It also has [the ability](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/628c83d5ab2706b0f64d69568c57a718ec7c5e2a/src/rust/engine/fs/src/store.rs#L168-L223) to download individual `Digest`s from the remote store to the local one.

We need to add the ability to download a `Directory`, and its contents, recursively.

This should probably take the form of a function named something like `ensure_local_has_recursive` which loads the top-level `Directory`, and drives a queue of `Digest`s to download using the relevant `load_bytes_*` functions.

issue_title: Xargs helper not working for JVM tasks
issue_body:
The Xargs helper (currently) works by catching `OSError`s with `errno.E2BIG`. But the current usage of Xargs for java process execution in `checkstyle`: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/6f88715b0b094e2839a9c84ff768546949337689/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py#L129-L135
will not work, because the exception is captured and converted at least twice (in `src/python/pants/java/executor.py` and `src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/nailgun_task.py`) on the way up.

Using caught exceptions is error prone, but `Xargs`' current implementation is probably optimal. Most likely the solution will be to push `Xargs` support way down into the `ProcessExecutor`, or into `src/python/pants/java/util.py`, so that the exception travels the minimum distance before being handled. Failing that, a Java integration test and a fixup of the exception fiddling would get us by for a while.

issue_title: CI failing due to google-java-format quietly running before lint
issue_body:
## Background

See #5614 and https://travis-ci.org/pantsbuild/pants/jobs/356953064 for context.

The CI was failing due to a lint error on a java file that was not edited in the PR. Meanwhile the linter passes on the file as is when run locally. 

It turns out that CI is running google-java-format before running the linter, and google-java-format is rewriting the file in a way that causes the linter to fail.

## Solution?

I think the immediate solution here is to disable google-java-format by default. A long term suggestion might be to add an option for the formatter itself to exit non-zero if an edit is made, so that a formatter could be more programmatically integrated into a CI pipeline.  Obviously since the formatter itself caused the linter to fail, this is probably not a good idea until we update the google-java-format config to better match the project and linter style guidelines.

issue_title: Audit memory usage
issue_body:
Although they're not perfect, allocation profiles from a few different phases during the lifecycle of `./pants list ::` indicate that the majority of both held and emphemeral allocation happens inside of `nodes::Context::get`, which is where `Graph` manipulation occurs.

Thought process:
  1) can we use raw references to a thing in order to avoid needing to own/clone it (without the lifetime management being a pain in the butt)?
  2) if it is impossible/impractical to hold a reference, is it large/copied enough to be worth moving into an `Arc`?
  3) if it needs to be owned, should it be in a Box in order to make the average size of an enum smaller?
  4) if it's ephemeral but occasionally large, should it move into a [thread local](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/thread/struct.LocalKey.html)?

A summary of `std::mem::size_of` (bytes) for a few relevant types:

    bytes	type
    24	DepSet
    24	Entries
    216	Entry
    16	EntryStateField
    24	Nodes
    16	NodeFuture<NodeResult>
    120	NodeKey
    24	ReadLink
    24	Stat
    24	Scandir
    72	Select
    112	SelectDependencies
    48	SelectLiteral
    88	SelectProjection
    48	Snapshot
    96	Task
    64	NodeResult
    80	Selector
    32	selector::Select
    72	selector::SelectDependencies
    48	selector::SelectProjection
    24	selector::SelectLiteral

Note: this is their _shallow_ size: if they contain a `Box` (and thus also`Arc`/`Vec`/`String`/etc), those are not counted. See [this post](https://blog.mozilla.org/nnethercote/2015/06/03/measuring-data-structure-sizes-firefox-c-vs-servo-rust/) for more info.

issue_title: Add returnMetaAttributes flag to freetext import API
Article fields in the wrong language are getting indexed
issue_body:
At [search_indexes.py](https://github.com/aldryn/aldryn-newsblog/blob/master/aldryn_newsblog/search_indexes.py) there are `safe_translation_getter()` calls without current language argument (and it looks like _parler_ does not try to detect the current language inside that method either). So, article's filelds translated in first available language are sometimes indexed in the _Solr_ core for another language.
The _aldryn-search_ was built with _hvad_ in mind, so we probably need to take some extra steps in index methods to properly index translatable models.
@yakky, can you try to reproduce this and adjust the index?
CC: @czpython @intellectronica

issue_title: Feature proposal: kubernetes secret push
issue_body:
I have a little app that listens for the Confidant webhook and on updates, pulls secrets from Confidant and converts to and places them into Kubernetes Secrets format on a cluster.
The naming of the Confidant Service and Credentials map by convention to k8s clusters and namespaces.

I'm wondering if this feature would be acceptable to be incorporated into Confidant itself, with an option to enable the functionality?

If this sounds interesting, I'd be happy to provide a PR to make it happen.

issue_title: Preserve mementos' scheme in CDXJ
issue_body: When generating the CDXJ in the indexer (note: NOT the CDXJ TimeMap), the URI-R of each memento is lost in the SURTing procedure. In #346 @ibnesayeed suggested we retain the scheme as a JSON attribute. Knowing the scheme of the URI-Ms will allow us to more accurately infer the scheme of the URI-R that is represented in TimeMaps, e.g., if all are http/https, the original can be populated with this respective scheme.

issue_title: malformed JSON: quotes in a string should be converted
issue_body:
in version 3.4.16

The below is a message in JSON format, which contains a string value for the "reason" key, and this string values has embedded quotes around the number 2 ("2"). 

{ "exabgp": "3.4.8", "time": 1466620850, "host" : "miab1", "pid" : "28251", "ppid" : "28221", "counter": 50, "type": "state", "neighbor": { "ip": "64.95.60.3", "address": { "local": "10.101.0.1", "peer": "64.95.60.3"}, "asn": { "local": "65015", "peer": "65015"}, "state": "down", "reason": "out loop, peer reset, message [notification sent (1,0)] error[Message header error / Unspecific / can not decode update message of type "2"]"} }

The above json can not be properly deserialized because of the incorrect delimiter expected after the doublequote preceding the 2 character in:

"out loop, peer reset, message [notification sent (1,0)] error[Message header error / Unspecific / can not decode update message of type "2"]"

For objects that must be converted to JSON, consider:
changing or removing quotes
inserting \\ before quotes (better)
using a JSON serializer, such as json.dumps (best)

(also note the incorrect version key,value "exabgp": "3.4.8". I'll log another bug for that.)

issue_title: Add helper API on top of core API
issue_body:
It seems that the current architecture of `docker-py` is to exactly mirror the API of docker, and not add any "helper functions" on top. I totally agree that the docker-py core should mirror the API of docker as closely as possible. However, I keep finding myself re-writing common helpers over and over when integrating docker-py with other code. 

Would the maintainers be opposed to adding a `helper` API on top of the existing core API that adds  common operations, such as checking if a container or image exists, converting raw strings into more pythonic types, etc? I'm happy to provide some initial PRs to build a base for this new API if someone gives me a green light

issue_title: Add default parameter metadata to NI-SCOPE
issue_body:
Go through the API with a domain expert, and decide for each function which parameters should have default values in the Python API and what those values should be.

This needs to be done carefully, because in Python, all parameters with default values must go at the end of the parameter list. So changing this later would likely be a source breaker for client code.

This will most likely be a subset of Recommended/Optional input terminals on corresponding LabVIEW VIs.

issue_title: When NI Switch Executive is added, need to add switch executive string buffer mechanism
issue_body:
* Description of issue

In Switch Executive, for strings, you:
1. create a pointer 
2. Assign a value to that pointer with the size of the string buffer you have pre-allocated
3. Pass the pointer into the dll 
4. Read pointer containing the actual length of the string

This is similar to the IVI dance, except

1. The length of the string is not returned from the function. Switch Executive returns the error code always. If I used ivi-dance as a mechanism, then the string size is always 0, resulting in an empty string.
2. The direction is out instead of in

To resolve this for Switch Executive, I had to do the following.

- Change type from pointer to VIInt32 array of size 1 (that is essentially a pointer.)
- Set direction from out, to in (so both I and the dll could set the value, as not setting it would cause no string)
- Set default value of "Size" to the default used in examples,
- Use "Size" to allocated the size of the string buffer.

This enables the string to be read, and partially hidden from the customer (via defaults).

We should create a mechanism for string buffers that use this modified IVI-Dance mechanism.

issue_title: Example Variables in Scenario Outlines description not showing up in Allure Report
issue_body:
#### I'm submitting a ... 
  - [ x] bug report
  - [ ] feature request

#### What is the current behavior?
If you include a [Variable in the Scenario outline](http://behave.readthedocs.io/en/latest/new_and_noteworthy_v1.2.5.html#name-may-contain-placeholders) it doesn't fill that variable in for the name of the test.

#### Steps:
A feature file that includes a Scenario Outline and examples. And uses the examples in the Scenario Outline name.
```
Feature: Not supported yet

  Scenario Outline: Wow <name>-<birthyear>
    Given ...
    Then ... 
    Examples:
    | name  | birthyear |
    | Alice |  1985     |
    | Bob   |  1975     |
```
Under the feature "Not supported yet" two test will appear named "`Wow <name>-<birthyear>`".

#### What is the expected behavior?
Two test would appear named: "`Wow Alice-1985`" & "`Wow Bob-1975`"

#### What is the motivation / use case for changing the behavior?
It does a better job of describing tests than example tags appearing to the right of the title.

#### Please tell us about your environment:
- Allure version:        2.6.0
- Test framework:     behave@1.2.6
- Allure adaptor:      allure-behave@2.3.2b1

issue_title: Log size limit settings?
issue_body:
Log files stored in /var/lib/deis/store/logs are not limited in size. I've recently had a problem with Ceph deadlock and while investigating, noticed that one of my log files was 18GB large. Looks like there are two improvements that can be made here:

1. There should be configuration option for logger to specify maximum number of log entries (or maximum file size), just like Heroku's 1500 lines limit. This is to avoid huge files on disk. Another option here is some kind of logrotate.

2. There should be configuration option or hardcoded value for maximum logs rate from an app, bytes/second. This is to avoid deadlocks in Ceph MDS when application with multiple containers on several hosts generates high amount of logs.

Please let me know if it makes sense.

issue_title: Use secrets storage for environment variables
issue_body:
Deis currently lets users set environment variables for their apps. In Kubernetes, these should be stored as secrets, and then imported into the environment at runtime.

For non-buildpack apps, they can be mounted as secrets volumes, but do not necessarily need to be placed into env vars.

issue_title: Lag for agg_autocorrelation, misleading documentation
issue_body: In the documentation it is said that `agg_autocorrelation` computes the autocorrelations for all lags up to the length of the series. But looking at the codes, this is not the case: The `nlags` parameter of the `acf` function is not defined, and it defaults to 40. It would actually be nice if this lag could be defined as a parameter. Anyway, the docs are misleading, but I am not suggesting to change the lag to the length of the series as the long lags are estimated inefficiently and they dominate the aggregation.

issue_title: PeerSubscriber.msg_queue can get too long and slow sync to a crawl
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

When recently debugging slow sync issues I've seen the `ChainSyncer`'s msg_queue grow to more than 5k items and stay that long pretty constantly. I don't know what caused it to get that long (could be a malicious peer DoSing us, some blocking calls in the main thread that prevented it from consuming messages, or maybe just more connected peers than our processing power could handle), but once it gets that long the sync will pretty much stall as the main loop will timeout waiting for block data (that has already arrived but is at the end of the queue), re-request that data and go back to waiting for it. That means we'll end up downloading/processing the same data multiple times, and even just to detect whether it's duplicated or not, some processing is necessary, so the event loop never manages to catch up and process all pending messages.

### How can it be fixed

We probably need a smaller upper limit on the msg queue (current one is 10k), as well as dropping peer/msgs when we reach the limit (currently we'll just [raise a QueueFull error](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/issues/1032)).

We may also want to look into keeping track of average messages/second we receive from every peer, possibly disconnecting if it's above a certain limit. Or something more elaborate, with the goal of preventing malicious peers from DoSing us

issue_title: SEM-I
issue_body:
(edit: rewriting entire issue as it described the VPM, not the SEM-I)

The Semantic Interface a description of the semantic entities and relations allowed by the grammar (see http://moin.delph-in.net/ErgSemantics/Interface)

The first step of integrating SEM-I info into pyDelphin is just loading and querying the data, e.g. predicates, variable properties, variable types, etc. Serializing the data (back to .smi format or to a dict for JSON output) would be part of this.

The second step would allow MRS operations (e.g. comparison) to use a SEM-I to do subsumption of predicates, instantiation of missing arguments (e.g. in DMRS->MRS conversion), and so on.

issue_title: Binary prefix in c++ strings
issue_body:
I spent some time struggling with the binary prefix contained in c++ string objects. Here ```NULL``` characters are not used to terminate strings and can be actual content of a string.

The solution I found is:
```
#define MYPREFIX "/some/path/with/_b_env_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_placehold_pla"
std::string p_wrong = MYPREFIX;
std::string p_correct = std::string(MYPREFIX).c_str();
```

In ```p_wrong``` the text is already placed in memory by the compiler and interpreted as a string of >255 characters. The correct version is casting the full text to a c string and calling the ```std::string``` constructor at runtime which is truncating correctly padding.

It is a pity that I had to patch the code for it (and the time invested in finding out the issue).

Is there another way for dealing with this issue?

issue_title: websocket inside of a thread
issue_body:
Hi,
I am building a package to communicate with a websocket-based API for a home automation system. I need to be able to send a command (say put the hub into pairing mode to add a new device) and wait to see if the device was added.

Right now I can create the websocket, send the request, then I get back ack that the request was successful. The script ends there.

I can use websocket-client and run_forever() but that is blocking and I cannot send to it.

I am playing with lomond and it looks like a nice package. Is there a way I can put the websocket in a thread so it is not blocking, but send to it if I want to do something?

Example:
1) Connect to the websocket
2) Send a "setActivePlace" request
Now I am good to go
3) Wait for an event that a motion sensor has been tripped
4) Send a payload to the websocket to do X
5) Receive confirmation that the request was sent
6) Wait for an event stating that X was completed
7) Repeat

issue_title: Unable to serialize set with terms filter query
issue_body:
Please refer [this issue](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/29047).
Basically the need is to have type casting for objects that can't be serialized. In this case that object is a **set** which should be type casted to a list.

issue_title: A bit too hidden "ignore" parameter.
issue_body:
I'm trying pylint today, and reviewing my code with it, it shows a `Unexpected keyword argument 'ignore' in method call [E1123]`, when I call the `indices.create` method.

And it seems legit, the create method is defined as:
```
    @query_params('timeout', 'master_timeout')
    def create(self, index, body=None, params=None):
        """
        Create an index in Elasticsearch.
        `<http://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/indices-create-index.html>`_

        :arg index: The name of the index
        :arg body: The configuration for the index (`settings` and `mappings`)
        :arg master_timeout: Specify timeout for connection to master
        :arg timeout: Explicit operation timeout
        """
```

There's no way here, even for the developper, to know that it's possible to give an `ignore` parameter here. [I know it's documented](https://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.org/en/master/api.html#ignore), does documenting it here is enough to completly hide its existance in the methode where it's possible to use it ?

issue_title: Urllib3HttpConnection should have separate client_cert and client_key arguments
issue_body:
Many deployments have the client cert and key in separate files, for security purposes.  You usually want much tighter control of who can read the key file.

Note that the function that is eventually called with these arguments is `ssl.wrap_socket()` https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/ssl.html#ssl.wrap_socket which has separate keyfile and certfile arguments (although certfile supports a file with both the cert and key in it).

If I append the ` --manual-integration` flag, then I get the following error instead (I assume specifying the GitHub token would also work?):

Finally, if I append `--branch master` to it, then it seems to succeed, since I can run `az appservice web source-control show` and see the correct settings.

issue_title: ClientConnectorError: "Too many open files" with ClientSession
issue_body:
## Long story short

I'm getting `ClientConnectorError: [Errno 24] Cannot connect to host <domain>:80 ssl:False [Too many open files]` when downloading from lots of different urls:
1. Is this an error with aiohttp, asyncio, python or me?
2. would be graet if `ClientConnectorError` could include attributes giving details on the error rather than just formatting the message.

Possibly related to or a duplicate of #1821 #1907 #764 #1667

## Expected (hoped for) behaviour

`ClientSession` to be able to make 100k's of requests to 100k's of hosts without intermittent errors occurring.

## Steps to reproduce

Download file from [here](https://gist.github.com/chilts/7229605) and extract to `top-1m.csv`, run

```python
import asyncio
import re
from itertools import groupby
from operator import itemgetter
from pathlib import Path
from statistics import mean

from aiohttp import ClientSession, TCPConnector, ThreadedResolver


class ResponseTimes:
    def __init__(self):
        self.loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
        connector = TCPConnector(
            verify_ssl=False,
            limit=200,
            resolver=ThreadedResolver(loop=self.loop),
        )
        self.session = ClientSession(loop=self.loop, connector=connector)
        self.results = []
        self.sem = asyncio.Semaphore(value=100, loop=self.loop)

    def run(self, sites):
        self.loop.run_until_complete(self._run(sites))
        self.session.close()

    async def _run(self, sites):
        coros = [self.get(url) for url in sites]
        start = self.loop.time()
        await asyncio.gather(*coros, loop=self.loop)
        print(f'total time taken: {self.loop.time() - start:0.3f}s')
        print(f'mean download length: {mean([r[1] for r in self.results]):0.4f}s')
        self.results.sort(key=itemgetter(0))
        print(f'   time   count   status')
        print('========================')
        for k, g in groupby(self.results, key=itemgetter(0)):
            g = list(g)
            print(f'{mean([r[1] for r in g]):0.4f}s    {len(g):4}      {k}')

    async def get(self, domain):
        url = 'http://' + domain
        async with self.sem:
            start = self.loop.time()
            try:
                async with self.session.get(url, timeout=5) as r:
                    status = r.status
                    # content_len = len(await r.read())
            except Exception as e:
                status = f'{e.__class__.__name__}: {e}'
                status = re.sub(f'[a-z0-9\-\.]+:(80|443)', r'<domain>:\1', status)
            else:
                status = str(status)
            time_taken = self.loop.time() - start
        self.results.append((status, time_taken))
        # print(f'{url:>40}: {time_taken:0.4f}s {status}')


top_text = Path('top-1m.csv').read_text()
limit = 2000
sites = []
for v in top_text.split('\n'):
    if not v:
        continue
    if len(sites) >= limit:
        break
    sites.append(v.split(',', 1)[1])
print(f'sites to get: {len(sites)}')

ResponseTimes().run(sites)
```
## Actual behaviour

Output of the script.

Note that of the 2000 requests, >800 returned a `ClientConnectorError` with `too many open files`

```
sites to get: 2000
Can not load response cookies: Illegal key '_csrf/link'
Can not load response cookies: Illegal key '_csrf/link'
total time taken: 30.159s
mean download length: 1.2121s
   time   count   status
========================
1.4887s     980      200
1.3857s       2      400
1.0834s      17      403
0.8338s      16      404
1.3580s       2      408
1.4277s       1      418
1.2045s       1      429
0.5417s       3      500
0.4710s       9      503
0.9899s       1      521
```

issue_title: Not waiting for connection_lost() when transport closed
issue_body:
## Long story short

aiohttp calls `close()` on the transport and then immediately discards it without waiting for `connection_lost()`. This is a problem for SSL transports, which have to shut down asynchronously, and it appears aiohttp only "works" with the standard library's SSL transport because of a bug in that transport.

## Expected behaviour

aiohttp waiting for connection_lost() before discarding transport

## Actual behaviour

It not doing so

## Steps to reproduce

    import aiohttp
    import asyncio

    async def main():
        conn = aiohttp.TCPConnector(verify_ssl=False)
        async with aiohttp.ClientSession(connector=conn) as session:
            async with session.get('https://httpbin.org/') as resp:
                resp.raise_for_status()

    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    try:
        loop.run_until_complete(main())
    finally:
        loop.close()

Run this program and breakpoint `connection_made()` and `connection_lost()` in client_proto.py. The former is called, but not the latter. 

The program appears to run successfully, but to convince you that something is severely amiss, add a `__del__` method to the class `_SelectorSocketTransport` in asyncio's selector_events.py and breakpoint it and that transport's `close()` method. You'll see that `close()` is never called and `__del__` gets called during shutdown, after the loop has been closed and the module finalised, because it still has a reader registered and is therefore leaked (while still waiting on I/O) in a reference cycle with the event loop.

If I'm understanding it right, the overall behaviour appears to be due to two bugs, one in asyncio and one in aiohttp:

1. When `close()` is called on the SSLProtocolTransport in asyncio's ssl_proto.py it initiates an asynchronous shutdown process, which should ultimately result in it calling `connection_lost()`. However, this doesn't progress for reasons I haven't fully figured out, so `connection_lost()` is never called. The shutdown process is clearly broken because it never results in `close()` being called on the underlying transport, which is leaked as shown above.
2. aiohttp doesn't wait for `connection_lost()`, so the hang that one would expect as a result of this never happens. By luck, the broken shutdown sequence never actually causes any errors.

I'm actually more interested in the latter, the aiohttp behaviour, because I've written my own SSL transport (based on pyopenssl), which does a correct asynchronous shutdown sequence. When aiohttp discards my transport without waiting for `connection_lost()`, it causes errors, which I don't think are my fault.

## Your environment

Tested on Windows and Linux with python 3.5.1 and 3.5.2 and aiohttp 2.0.7 and 2.1.0.

issue_title: Chardet 3.0 causes backwards incompatible changes
issue_body:
## Long story short
[Chardet 3.0 was released today](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/chardet). Aiohttp refers to chardet without a version boundary and so all new installs of aiohttp install Chardet 3.0. This is causing issues in aiohttp. This got caught by our test suite: https://travis-ci.org/home-assistant/home-assistant/jobs/221068366#L285

Aiohttp experienced similar problems with Yarl in the past. The solution for that was then to pin Yarl to a specific range.

## Expected behaviour

Short term: Chardet dependency to be limited to <3.

## Actual behaviour

Chardet 3.0 is getting installed.

## Steps to reproduce

```
git clone https://github.com/home-assistant/home-assistant.git
cd home-assistant
python3 -m venv .
source bin/activate
pip3 install tox
tox
```

## Your environment

Tests ran on Travis CI. Test failed on Python 3.4, 3.5, 3.6 and 3.6-dev.

## Workaround

Install `chardet==2.3` before installing aiohttp

issue_title: setup() should add the middleware
issue_body:
IMO, adding necessary middleware is the responsibility of a plugin's `setup()` function.

Current:
```python
import aiohttp_debugtoolbar
app = web.Application(middlewares=[aiohttp_debugtoolbar.middleware])
aiohttp_debugtoolbar.setup(app)
```

Proposed:

```python
import aiohttp_debugtoolbar
app = web.Application()
aiohttp_debugtoolbar.setup(app)
```

issue_title: Yum broken when RPM from this requirement with underscores.
issue_body:
Please never use underscores in python-packages names.

[PEP-008 conatins](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/#package-and-module-names)
> Python packages should also have short, all-lowercase names, although the use of underscores is discouraged.

We have a really problem. When our build system convert all dependencies to RPM (using [FPM](https://github.com/jordansissel/fpm)) yum is broken. We are can't use this package in our projects.

issue_title: import_data with `None` value
issue_body:
I'am expected such new test to pass:

```
# test_models.py

def test_model_import_data_nullify():
    class User(Model):
        name = StringType()
        age = IntType()
        votes = IntType(default=0)

    user = User(dict(name="Ivan", age=18))
    user.import_data(dict(age=None))
    assert user.age is None

    assert user.votes == 0
    user.import_data(dict(votes=5))
    assert user.votes == 5

    user.import_data(dict(votes=None))
    assert user.votes == 0

    user.import_data(dict(votes=0))
    assert user.votes == 0
```

So, if we explicitly provide `None` as value for some model field, then set to default value or to `None`. 
But, with current code, `None` values are just ignored and the value remains the same after `import_data`.

To make this test pass, all is needed is to modify code in [`import_data`](https://github.com/schematics/schematics/blob/bff12fc8cdb7f86ad977035eb033f5f4ca01da2d/schematics/models.py#L237) method:

    def import_data(self, raw_data, **kw):
        """
        Converts and imports the raw data into the instance of the model
        according to the fields in the model.

        :param raw_data:
            The data to be imported.
        """
        data = self.convert(raw_data, **kw)
        for k in data.keys():
           if data[k] is None and k not in raw_data:
               del data[k]
        self._data.update(data)
        return self

All other tests will still pass. I can make a pull request, if it not break some architecture decisions.

issue_title: Give the RNASeq CGL pipeline an option to take paired fastq files as inputs rather than tarballs
issue_body:
Here are the reasons why we (the production team) would like this:
1. If we provide the two fastq files as inputs, we can verify that they're roughly the same size and that the names seem to match, which would allow us to identify problem inputs earlier.  If the fastqs are packaged in tarballs, these details are hidden.
2. The batch alignment pipeline takes paired fastqs.  If we can take the same type of inputs consistently, across pipelines, that will simplify production greatly!

issue_title: setup.py should not declare Toil as install_requires
issue_body:
So I've thought about this intensely. I think it is wrong for toil-script's `setup.py` to refer to toil via `install_requires`. There is always the danger that when the user installs toil-scripts (especially when installing into a virtualenv), that a different version of Toil is pulled in via that dependency. It may be a different version of Toil than what the user has on the system, and it will be installed without extras while the user's version was installed with extras.

Instead `setup.py` should contain an explicit version check, which I will outline shortly.

issue_title: flake8 and coverage interaction slows things down unnecessarily
issue_body:
We are noticing that enabling flake8 and coverage testing with pytest is slowing test execution down by a factor of 4. In one example, the tests run in 7 seconds without either plugin. Adding coverage slows down test execution to 13 seconds. Adding flake8 (no coverage) is 15 seconds, and using both is 30 seconds.

Is there any way that coverage can be disabled for flake8 testing? Or is coverage enabled for everything in the python process (including pytest itself) so it's fundamentally impossible to pick and choose which tests have coverage enabled?

While switching from plain coverage to pytest-cov I noticed that the *multiple sources* option isn't really supported because `nargs` is set to `?`. Any thoughts about this?

issue_title: Possible optimization in Worker
issue_body: In remote.py WorkerInteractor.pytest_collectreport the collectreport event is sent to the master which I believe handles this in dsession.py:worker_collectreport. It appears that this message is only meaningful if rep.failed is true. I am running pytest on a machine with 16 cpus on a very large test suite. The number of collectreport events actually causes the e2e time of running the test to be slower when using 16 cpus compared to using 8. When I pruned the number of collectreport events sent from workers to the master I was able to reduce the collection time from ~120 seconds to ~32 seconds.  Not sure what the priority of this should be but figured I would mention it,

issue_title: Make letsencrypt-auto more paranoid.
issue_body:
Make letsencrypt-auto use PyPI (where we'll put our dev releases from now on), a signed requirements file, and peep.

Prereq: #1571.

* letsencrypt-auto will go figure out what the latest version of LE is (from PyPI or GH--over HTTPS to make it harder to artificially keep LE out of date) then wget that version's reqs.txt and signature off GH.
* It'll verify the sig with `openssl` (already in the bootstrap scripts and easy to airgap sigs for).
* Then it'll `peep install` that reqs.txt into the venv.

issue_title: [LE.org/howitworks] Unavailable switch mentioned in documentation
issue_body:
In the [How It Works: Renewing a Certificate](https://letsencrypt.org/howitworks/#renewing-a-certificate) on the LE homepage, the switch `--keep-until-renewal` is mentioned. However, this switch isn't available in the LE client, it's called `--keep-until-expiring`. This unfortunately apparently [confuses people](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/renew-not-working/9383).

I can't find the content of that page anywhere on GitHub; otherwise I would have made a PR.. :stuck_out_tongue:

issue_title: Config for each layer causes huge notebooks

issue_title: There are no exception during parse wrong year in datetime string
issue_body:
Parser will not raise exceptions If you specify wrong year "201A".
Sample code:
```
from dateutil import parser

origin = "201A-01-01T23:58:39.239769+03:00"
parsed = parser.parse(origin)
print("origin={}".format(origin))
print("parsed={}".format(parsed.isoformat()))
```
Output:
origin=201A-01-01T23:58:39.239769+03:00
parsed=2017-03-22T23:58:39.239769+03:00

I use python3.5 on Mac.
dateutil version="2.6.0"

issue_title: Support Python3
issue_body:
It's great that the tool doesn't require Python 3 and still supports Python 2.7, but since it's a brand new development and Python 3 has been around for ages, it should support both.

In practice, this isn't that big a deal and just means doing things like using print functions (with `from __future__ import print_function`) instead of the 2.x style print keyword. Besides the print function, the other main incompatibility is division https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0238/

issue_title: support parallel thread execution
issue_body: It would be nice to support parallel thread execution, and I would be happy to contribute to that. How big of an effort do you guys think this would be? Suggestions welcome :)

issue_title: dockerPull field in DockerRequirement should pull image updates before running
issue_body: I have been running some CWL workflows that utilize the DockerRequirement "dockerPull" field, and when the Docker image in question is not present locally, the field works as intended, pulling the image first before running. However, if the image is updated in the Docker registry sometime later, the "dockerPull" field will continue to run the local image with no actual attempt to do a "docker pull" for updates. This requires me to have to do a manual "docker pull" on the command line before invoking the CWL. Is it possible to have CWL perform an actual "docker pull" every time the workflow is invoked with the "dockerPull" field (to grab the latest image update if available), as opposed to simply doing a "docker run" if the image already exists locally?

issue_title: No ckeditor_uploader browse view results when using google cloud storage
issue_body: Not sure if this should be possible or not but using google cloud storage, uploading works perfectly but the browse view returns nothing. Briefly experimented with bucket permissions to no avail. Any idea if this should be expected to work or is GCS simply incompatible with that feature? Since S3 is mentioned explicitly in the docs, is this known to work with that?

issue_body:
Hello,

I just tested todeay’s update to Django 1.11 and noticed that I get an exception when there’s a form with a CKEditor from django-ckeditor:

> TypeError at …
> build_attrs() got an unexpected keyword argument 'name'

> virtualenvs/cert/lib/python3.4/site-packages/ckeditor/widgets.py in render

`111        final_attrs = self.build_attrs(attrs, name=name)`

In 1.10 the same code works without any problem.

issue_title: Thumbnail uploaded image, and using the thumbnail separate from the content
issue_body:
Hello, thanks for making this awesome app. 
It meets all my requirements, but I was hoping if there's a way I can use the user posted image separate from the content. Currently, all the contents(image+text) are shown as {{content}} but I want to use {{post.image}} if it's possible. Could you please tell me if there's any way I can achieve this this functionality with django-ckeditor?

issue_title: IPython Magic Based on folium
issue_body:
I've started experimenting with some IPython magic to make it easier for non-programmers to embed maps in Jupyter notebooks. It uses `folium` to to generate the maps.

Code here: https://github.com/psychemedia/ipython_magic_folium

issue_title: features.GeoJson Tooltip
issue_body:
Noticing in the latest [documentation](https://python-visualization.github.io/folium/docs-master/modules.html?highlight=chor#folium.features.GeoJson) that there is now a `tooltip` option in the `folium.features.GeoJson` class. 

Right now it looks like it only takes raw strings - would it be possible to borrow some syntax from `style_function` and `highlight_function` in this class to make calls back to the GeoJson object via a `tooltip = lambda feature: feature['properties'][value]` structure, or to possibly wrap this into the highlight_function mouseover event dictionary? A popup in this same vein would be really functional as well. 

I'm new to JS, but going through the leaflet tutorials right now. I've never made a PR before, but really enjoy this library and would love to contribute if someone could steer me in the right direction to start.

Thank you!

issue_title: Missing styling documentation
issue_body:
1. `Folium` rocks! Thx so much for working on it!

2. I'm exploring it now, and trying to style some GeoJSON was much harder than expected, largely because I couldn't find any documentation on expected property names (for `folium`, I know there aren't for GeoJSON). After a while I've sniffed into the code, and saw that e.g. `fillColor` should work. But then `strokeColor` doesn't (`color` does). I'm not sure this is very consistent and it's totally unclear to me which convention or standard is expected here?

issue_body: https://watson-api-explorer.mybluemix.net/apis/discovery-v1

issue_title: latest_version should not be used in class
issue_body: In discovery the `latest_version` should not be used in the methods.

issue_title: Core path does not support swagger basePath
issue_body:
Related to #69.

Flask allows configuring the `APPLICATION_ROOT`, which provides the base path for the routes. #70 fixed this issue so that the flask extension reports the fully qualified path.

When the specs are dumped as swagger specs using `to_dict()`, the full path is shown on every route, even though the swagger specification supports the [`basePath`](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#fixed-fields) field on the root object.

I think it is important to keep the meaningful portion of the route URL separate from the base path to maintain readable documentation. #70 was the right fix for the flask extension since the `apispec` core assumes paths are absolute, but the fact that both `flask` and `swagger` support a configurable base path indicates that the `apispec` core should probably internalize the concept of base paths and expose them to extensions.

Examples of base path in similar libraries:

 - [Bottle: ResourceManager (default) path](http://bottlepy.org/docs/dev/api.html#bottle.ResourceManager)
 - [RAML: Base URI](https://github.com/raml-org/raml-spec/blob/master/versions/raml-10/raml-10.md/#base-uri-and-base-uri-parameters)

> Edit: Updated to refect that generating swagger specs is a core functionality of `apispec`, not an extension.

issue_title: add private helper function for redundant task of preparing newton inputs
issue_body:
**problem**
there is WET code in singlediode_methods where the inputs to i_from_v and v_from_i are manipulated for the newton method

**solution**
according to [this comment](https://github.com/pvlib/pvlib-python/pull/409#discussion_r199302424) here's a nice snippet:

```python
def _prepare_newton_inputs(i_or_v, args, v0):
        size, shape = _get_size_and_shape((i_or_v,) + args)
        if size > 1:
            args = [np.asarray(arg) for arg in args]
        # newton uses initial guess for the output shape
        # copy v0 to a new array and broadcast it to the shape of max size
        if shape is not None:
            v0 = np.broadcast_to(v0, shape).copy()
        else:
            v0 = voc_est
    return args, v0
```

**alternatives**
do nothing

**Additional context**
see #409

issue_body:
It is very convenient to 
```
./run.py save_config with x=123
./run.py with config.json
```

but this does not work with ingredients
```
./run.py save_config with x=123 data.y=123
./run.py with config.json
```

(data is an ingredient)
resulting in
`KeyError: 'Added a new config entry "data.y" that is not used anywhere'`

This is probably because you cannot override ingredient config values in the main experiment config (or at least I don't know how to do it).

issue_title: Wrong bias returned for denormal floating point numbers
issue_body:
The attached [example](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/388462/fp_bias.zip) constructs the largest denormal 32 bit floating point number and checks the returned exponent with Z3's API functions, e.g. with Z3_fpa_get_numeral_exponent_string. 

As a 32bit FP has a bias of 127, we'd expect to receive -127; however, Z3 returns -126. If we use an exponent of 2 (as a counter-check), we correctly receive -125.

As far as we have looked into the code, there seems to be a special case in [api_fpa.cpp](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/blob/b7de813c6386a5625c5570c99d8daf0b00e245f8/src/api/api_fpa.cpp#L1025), where mk_min_exp is used to compute the exponent for denormal numbers. However, [that computation](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/blob/b7de813c6386a5625c5570c99d8daf0b00e245f8/src/util/mpf.cpp#L1742-L1746) seems to correctly compute the result first, and then adds another 1; at least, we cannot see the reason for doing that last addition.

issue_title: How to let double variable do bit operations in Z3?
Optimizer returns a model that violates one of the constraints (ints+reals theory)
issue_body:
Here is a distilled example:
```python
import sys
print('python version', sys.version)
from z3 import *
print('z3 version', tuple(z3util.get_z3_version()))

opt = Optimize()

u0 = Real('u0')
u1 = Real('u1')
b0 = Int('b0')
b1 = Int('b1')
t1 = Real('t1')
t2 = Real('t2')

opt.add(u0 >= 0)
opt.add(u0 <= b0)
opt.add(u1 >= 0)
suspicious_constraint = u1 <= b1
opt.add(suspicious_constraint)
opt.add(t1 >= 10 * b0)
opt.add(t2 >= t1 + 10 * b1)
opt.add(10.1 <= 0.57 * u1)
opt.add(0.57 * u1 <= 0.525 * u0)

opt.minimize(t2)

print(opt.check())
m = opt.model()
print(m)
print('Is constraint', suspicious_constraint, 'satisfied?')
print(m.eval(suspicious_constraint))
```

And this is what I get:
```
python version 3.6.1 (v3.6.1:69c0db5, Mar 21 2017, 17:54:52) [MSC v.1900 32 bit (Intel)]
z3 version (4, 5, 1, 6898)
sat
[b0 = 20,
 u1 = 1010/57,
 u0 = 20,
 b1 = 17,
 t1 = 200,
 t2 = 21500/57]
Is constraint u1 <= ToReal(b1) satisfied?
False
```

I observed this behavior in the z3-4.4.1-x86-win stable, z3-4.5.0-x86-win stable, and even in the recent [4.5.1 nightly](https://github.com/Z3Prover/bin/blob/6f53bc404cb69979b973037dd78fd8d13e347158/nightly/z3-4.5.1.d3320f8b8143-x86-win.zip).

issue_title: With PbEq constraints, sometimes z3 returns unsound model
issue_body:
In Z3Py, the following code returns a model where the variable d is True and the other variables are False. This model doesn't satisfy the first constraint.

```
S = Solver()
a,b,c,d,e = Bools("a b c d e")
S.add(If(a, PbEq([(b,1),(c,1)], 3), PbEq([(b,1),(c,1)], 1)))
S.add(If(c, PbEq([(a,1),(d,1),(e,1)], 3), PbEq([(a,1),(d,1),(e,1)], 1)))
S.check()
M = S.model()
```

Z3Py agrees that the model it returned is unsound:

```
for constraint in S.assertions():
    print M.eval(constraint), # prints False True
```

I think I can replicate this [on rise4fun](https://rise4fun.com/Z3/20Li). If I understand the SMT2 language correctly, this encodes the same problem as above. The solver returns a model where c, d, and e are True, and a and b are False. This doesn't satisfy the second constraint.

issue_body:
I compiled coreutils 8.27. In the **cp** program, function 0x402FB0 (target_directory_operand), CFGFast doesn't recover the CFG correctly.  Below is the binary:
[cp.zip](https://github.com/angr/angr/files/1262088/cp.zip)

The error is a very simple one. Here is the CFG recovered by angr:
![screenshot from 2017-08-29 17-36-44](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2527873/29850200-bd7c2cd4-8ce0-11e7-8db1-cccec109b99f.png)

As you can see, there is an edge missing in the angr's CFG, from node 0x402FEA to node 0x402FF1. Angr's analysis basically put a duplicated node 0x402FF1 inside the node 0x402FFA (since the first one contains return statement). The node 0x402FEA suppose to be 7 byte instead of 18.

issue_title: Better error reporting for unexpected 400 status
issue_body:
During development, tests will fail when the app returns Bad Request instead of the expected 200 or 201 code.  The error message from webtest only shows that 400 != 200, but with django forms or django-rest-framework validators, there is more information in the response body, but it’s not displayed.

Current fix: change the test to have status=400 and print response.body.

Desired change: Define DjangoTestApp._check_status:

```
    def _check_status(self, status, res):
        if status is not None and status != res.status_int:
            msg = 'Bad response: {} (not {})\n{}'.format(res.status, status, res.body)
            raise webtest.AppError(msg)
        super(DjangoTestApp, self)._check_status(status, res)
```

(Code adapted from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/13505778/finding-the-real-error-in-a-webtest-test-failure )

issue_title: Possible to load an external library?
issue_body:
Hello,

My apologies if this is not the right place; I didn't see a mailing list and the main hit I get is the [intro blog post](https://blog.sqreen.io/embedding-javascript-into-python/) which didn't seem the best, either.

To try and keep it brief, there's [a desire](https://community.plot.ly/t/use-plotly-offline-to-save-chart-as-image-file/408) to export image files from `plotly` without having to go through their servers. The functionality exists in their library, but one has to open the `.html` in a browser to trigger the `downloadImage()` function.

Due to this, solutions are somewhat hokey, relying on `selenium` and `phantomjs` to fake a browser session and either screenshot the page or trigger the download. I'm wondering if `py_mini_racer` would allow for calling the `js` code directly without having to do this.

I'm a `python` noob, and even more so for `js`. I *think* what I'm asking is, "Can I do the equivalent of this with `py_mini_racer`"?
```
<head>
  <script src="https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-latest.min.js"></script>
</head>
```

Then, I could do something [like this](https://plot.ly/javascript/getting-started/), replicating what would normally be inside of `<script>`:
```
ctx.eval("""
Plotly.plot( "div_name", [{
	x: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
	y: [1, 2, 4, 8, 16] }], {
	margin: { t: 0 } } );
"""
```

Am I understanding this library correctly?

issue_title: Wrong behavior in compressed version
issue_body:
I'm testing `django-jet` as `OpenEdx`'s admin theme and i have the correct behaviour in `LMS` section:
![seleccion_018](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5034215/26670642/0fb4a9fc-46bb-11e7-9df2-c5eb5f758578.png)
It works perfectly in `Opera - Chromiun` and `Firefox`, and, very important, as you can see, all static files isn't compressed.

But in `Studio / CMS` the history is very different:
![seleccion_016](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5034215/26670634/0867bf36-46bb-11e7-996f-fdddf1b345f1.png)
The styles are a melty of normal `Django` admin and `Jet` and make very ugly effects:
- Pinned button right padding is wrong.
- Header / Breadcrumbs section with normal styles.
- User button and menu dissapeared.
- Wrong `svg` icons rendering.
And others.
As you can see this site use compressor for compress the files.

![seleccion_017](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5034215/26670637/0c04c6c0-46bb-11e7-8c14-20b9786e7ed5.png)
So, i proceeded to delete the base admin style and i got a "perfect" behaviour, but i know this is not properly solution.

In `Firefox` the problem is not uglier than `Opera - Chromiun` but exist also:
![seleccion_020](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5034215/26670646/1534f2a6-46bb-11e7-80db-2a4745bae785.png)
As you can see, again, title section in menu doesn't appear and the user name button with menu also.
![seleccion_023](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5034215/26670648/18def38e-46bb-11e7-9968-e3b6cb14b566.png)
Deleting the same line of code (`admin-base`) the theme works "perfectly".

So, i think as conclusion that for any reason `Jet` styles are conflicting with normal styles with both are compressed.

Why happen this?

Additional information here:
![seleccion_024](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5034215/26671448/3690394e-46be-11e7-9dc3-9a1183c85a7e.png)

issue_title: Use random data generation library
issue_body:
Let's use a proper library for generating random data. The intent is two-fold. First, I'd like to get rid of functions like this (from `pulp_smash.tests.platform.api_v2.test_user`):

```python
def _rand_str():
    """Return a randomized string."""
    return type('')(randint(-100000, 100000))
```

Second, having a good random data generation library lowers the friction for writing new tests. It's one less worry for a developer.

issue_title: Change Pulp-Smash Code layout to fit Pulp2 and Pulp3 needs.
issue_body:
Code related to Pulp3 will be added to Pulp-Smash soon.

Based on that it does make sense to modify pulp-smash current layout to fit this need. Below, a draft version of future layout .

```
pulp_smash/
├── constants.py
├── pulp2
│   ├── constants.py
│   └── tests
└── pulp3
    ├── constants.py
    └── tests
```

Every project will have its own dir, and it is related files. All the other files there a common for both projects will be in a level above.  For instance, the constant files for Pulp3 will have all related constants of Pulp3. Constants shared by both projects, like pulp-fixtures ones, will be in another constant file a level above.

**This change in layout possibly will impact imports in different parts of the code, and how certain Jenkins Jobs are currently set.**

issue_title: Add ability to fiddle with services Pulp depends on
issue_body:
Let's add some mechanism(s) that let us fiddle with the services that Pulp depends on. Pulp is designed to be resilient against failure of certain services, and it would be nice if we could start or stop services at run-time to test that resilience.

At its simplest, the mechanism would look like this:

```python
def twiddle_service(state, services):
    # determine if remote system has `systemctl` available, or some other service manager
    # ssh some_user@remote_system systemctl state ' '.join(services)
```

One design consideration is what to do if the service you'd like to test might go by multiple names. For example, the message broker might be either RabbitMQ or Qpid. Do we make the user explicitly name the service to be twiddled, or do we provide abstraction? Another question is what to do if a non-zero return code is received. Do we raise an exception? (No, really. systemctl has a range of valid non-zero return codes.)

issue_title: Help with Mutation
issue_body:
Hello, I am trying to experiment with a simple mutation, here is my schema:

```
import graphene


class Person(graphene.ObjectType):

    name = graphene.String()
    age = graphene.Float()
    id = graphene.Int()

# Database of Person(s)
p1 = Person()
p2 = Person()

p1.id = 1
p1.age = 34
p1.name = 'Jack'

p2.id = 2
p2.age = 31
p2.name = 'Adam'

persons = [p1, p2]

class Query(graphene.ObjectType):

    # Create a field on which we can query and the attributes
    # we allow to query on
    person = graphene.Field(Person, id=graphene.Int())
    def resolve_person(self, args, info):
        for p in persons:
            if p.id == args.get('id'):
                return p

class UpdatePerson(graphene.Mutation):
    # Result field
    # XXX: This doesn't work
    # person = graphene.Field(Person)

    # This does work
    person = graphene.String()

    # The input fields
    class Input:
        name = graphene.String()
        id = graphene.Int()

    @classmethod
    def mutate(cls, instance, args, info):
        name = args.get('name')
        for p in persons:
            if p.id == args.get('id'):
                p.name = name
                return UpdatePerson(person=p.name)

class UpdatePersonMutation(graphene.ObjectType):
    updatePerson = graphene.Field(UpdatePerson)

schema = graphene.Schema(query=Query, mutation=UpdatePersonMutation)

```

In ``UpdatePerson`` above, when I have the person field as ``person = graphene.Field(Person)``, ( and correspondingly ``return UpdatePerson(person=p)``), upon sending a mutation query, I get: ``[GraphQLError('Field "person" of type "Person" must have a sub selection.',)]``. 

Any suggestions as to what I am doing wrong?

issue_title: Universal Sentence Encoder not using GPU
issue_body:
I've been trying to figure out why is it that the `https://tfhub.dev/google/universal-sentence-encoder/1` module is choking the GPU ram and not using it. 

On one of a local machine with CUDA 9.0 Ubuntu 16.04, it's just not using any GPU compute when I did:

```python
import tensorflow as tf
import tensorflow_hub as hub

embed = hub.Module(model_name_dan)

with tf.Session() as session:
    session.run([tf.global_variables_initializer(), tf.tables_initializer()])
```

issue_title: Add --version
issue_body:
I apologize for adding three specific feature requests to a single issue, but since they're all related, and there's a corresponding screen shot, I thought it might be best here.

Also, I like the idea of the tool emitting Markdown compatible files, but since it's new and unexpected behavior, I would advocate that one needs a flag to explicitly enable it.

# Screenshot
Top screen `jasmine` is running an older version of `csvlook`. By inspecting the file `/usr/local/bin/version` I think this is csvkit 0.9.1. Fails when trying to use unknown flag ``--no-inference` instead of ignoring the flag.

Bottom screen `belle` doesn't have a version, and it's not clear from inspecting the python source. But it's recent as of yesterday. 

The CSV file in question is 511 rows x 546 cols (a mix of strings, ints, floats and booleans)

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/280700/22307094/988bf48e-e2f6-11e6-8fb0-fc6cb14e9bb4.png)

# My requests
1. Add `--version`. It'll be hard to report bugs if there's not an easy way to query what version is installed. Also, if there was a reliable `--version` flag, then we could query at shell login and set appropriate wrappers to enable `

2. Make `--no-inference` the default state. So one would use `--inference` to enable it.

3. Inference is *slow*. In the second screenshot, it takes 10 seconds to complete, vs. 1 second disabled. The older version (in `jasmine`) was even faster at 1/2 second.

I love the tool...I probably use `csvlook` 50-100 times *per day*. Across 6 machines (I do a lot with CSV files). 

# My hack solution
Fork `csvkit` and disable inference by default. If I get this working, I'll submit pull request, but it would be a surprise to your new users that _expect_ the inference to be enabled by default. 

Maybe there's an *environment* variable that can control all this?

issue_title: Custom owning pointer
Cannot get std::vector from binded member function having passed by reference std::vector as parameter
issue_body:
I have a dll `Core`, `class __declspec(dllexport)`'ing a certain class `X`. This class is in charge of some progressive calculation, successive values being kept track of in an "array". (I don't know yet which `c++`/`python` structure is the best for what I am trying to achieve.) This "array" can either be 1) a member of `X` or 2) returned by a member function of `X`. I am using this dll in another `pyd` dll, `Binder`, which as its name indicates, is in charge of doing python bindings of `X` (`c++`) member functions. Then I `import` the `Binder.pyd` dll in a python file, and I try to use python functions.

At the beginning, the "array" wasn't a member of `X`, which had a function `void F(std::vector<double> & array)` in charge of updating the array, but any run of the python file using the binded python version of `F` was triggering an access violation, precisely whenever `resize` or `push_back` where called on the `std::vector<double>`. I binded btw `std::vector<double>` as follows :

	py::class_<std::vector<double>>(m, "stdvectordouble")
		.def(py::init<>())
		.def("clear", &std::vector<double>::clear)
		.def("pop_back", &std::vector<double>::pop_back)
		.def("push_back", (void (std::vector<double>::*)(const double &)) &std::vector<double>::push_back)
		.def("__len__", [](const std::vector<double> &v) { return v.size(); })
		.def("__iter__", [](std::vector<double> &v) {

I tried `std::share_ptr<double>` + `int` (size) instead, same type of issue as well. I suspect that my problem is ultimately caused by this :

https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/172396/you-may-experience-an-access-violation-when-you-access-an-stl-object-t

though I don't really know how to put the proposed solution in practice in my case.

Remark : at the origin, `Core` and `Binder` dll's were one single dll, and I wasn't having the acces violation error triggered at run time.

issue_title: setup.py does not list all the headers that should be installed
issue_body:
`setup` function in the `setup.py` file has a `header` keyword argument used to pass the list of all the C++ header files that should be installed within the package.

Currently the following files are not listed:
* `chrono.h`
* `eval.h`
* `options.h`

and every build fails.

A possible solution to fix this issue is the definition of a function that automatically list all the header files within the `include/pybind11` directory.

issue_title: No more relative path connection from live-crt to archive-crt files
issue_body:
I just generated a new certificate with certbot in Docker from quay.io/letsencrypt/letsencrypt with the command 
```
docker run -it --rm -p 80:80 -p 443:443 \
-v $(pwd)/le:/etc/letsencrypt \
-v $(pwd)/le/lib:/var/lib/letsencrypt \
-v $(pwd)/le/log:/var/log/letsencrypt \
quay.io/letsencrypt/letsencrypt \
certonly \
--standalone \
--agree-tos \
--rsa-key-size 4096 \
--email EMAILADDR \
example.com
``` 

Last time I generated new certificates, there were relative symlinks from the certs in ./live/example.com/ to ./archive/example.com/ (e.g. `cert.pem -> ../../archive/www.example.com/cert1.pem`) created. But with the newest version, there are absolute paths for the symlinks (e.g. `cert.pem -> /etc/letsencrypt/archive/sub.example.com/cert1.pem`). The problem here is the usage of the (absolute) directory structure of the container, but on my host machine, the folder with the config and cert files is stored (and used) in a different location. 

Are the absolute symlink paths desired behaviour and/ or is it possible to switch back to the old relative path behaviour (e.g. via an input param)? I assume, that ./live and ./archive cannot be placed too far away from each other. 

* Version: certbot 0.10.0.dev0
* Docker-Image: quay.io/letsencrypt/letsencrypt   latest              84aa8c51b7e1
* Ubuntu 14.04

To sum up: I would expect relative paths from the certs symlinks in ./live/* to the original files in ./archive/* but now there are absolute paths for the symlinks.


As I can examine, it could be a connection to https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/1483
```
self.namespace.config_dir = os.path.abspath(self.namespace.config_dir)
self.namespace.work_dir = os.path.abspath(self.namespace.work_dir)
self.namespace.logs_dir = os.path.abspath(self.namespace.logs_dir)
```

8 Y ahora guarde el archivo

issue_title: Add off-chain settlement period negotiation
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The node opening the channel is the one responsible to chose the settlement period, ideally the settlement period chosen is good enough for both nodes and both nodes proceed to use the channel normally.

But, since the settlement period is configurable, the creator may be temped to use a lower settlement period to reduce the waiting time to withdraw the token from the channel, if the creator chooses a settlement period to low it may inadvertently create an unusable channel, since the counterparty may just ignore the channel considering the settlement period unsafe. 

## Solution

This may be fixed on-chain, allowing the nodes to *increase* settlement period while the channel is *opened*, the drawback for the instantiation is that this require transactions to be executed (note that it may still be useful to support this operation to extend the maximum number of possible hops in a mediation path).

Additionally this may be done off-chain, exchanging setup configuration among the nodes *prior* to the channel instantiation.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Add a protocol message to inform a counterparty that a new channel will be opened and its properties
- [ ] Add a second message for the counterparty to inform the minimum and maximum valid configuration values, for the time only the settlement period seems to be required.

issue_title: Speedup --blockchain-type=geth tests in the CI.
ReplacementTransactionUnderpriced during fixture setup
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

During the cron job on Saturday there was a failure of the token network fixture setup. The entire log can be seen [here](https://gist.github.com/LefterisJP/fa0fa511cb12c833097b53f25e7f0156).

It seems that there were multiple calls to the deposit token transaction.

## Task

Find out why that could happen and implement a fix. If possible also reproduce this in a test.

## Possible cause

There can be a race between multiple `TokenNetwork` or `TokenNetworkRegistry` instances especially since we now have [RPC I/O](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/39c73c613d1e04fd53f1ad2f485bd39c8d9c476e/raiden/network/proxies/token_network.py#L72) inside the proxy constructors which introduces a context switch.

So it's possible that two different code paths race down to the `TokenNetwork` (or any other proxy constructor) and we end up making two different transactions with the same nonce which would manifest itself like the attached log with a `ReplacementTransactionUnderpriced`.

Using a lock would possibly fix this.

issue_title: Validate all address input for EIP55 compatibility
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

We are now using web3.py which uses [EIP55](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-55.md) checksummed addresses/.

At the moment we sanitize addresses by using `to_checksum_address()` before passing them on to web3.py.

This is missing the point as we basically don't really validate the checksums on the time of input.

## TODO

- Validate all hex address input by the user for EIP55 compliance
    - Validate hex addresses given through the API endpoints [here](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/203c77df4e44bac52b5cee8cded2d57c557bbe95/raiden/api/v1/encoding.py#L46)
    - Validate hex addresses given through the cli
- Remove all `to_checksum_address` uses. If a hex address somehow gets to web3.py without already being checksummed it means we are not sanitizing some input.

issue_title: Enforcement of server restrictions by SoS
issue_body:
Our cluster has a no-large-job-on-head-node policy but we will have to use head nodes for all data communications (`rsync`) and job preparation, submission etc.

In practice it is easy to adopt a one-server-multi-config approach like this

```
hosts:
    pbs_head:
        address: cluster_address
        queue_type: process
    pbs:
        address: cluster_address
        queue_type: pbs
        template_file: pbs.tmpl
        ....
```

and use these two queues as follows

```
sos run script rsync_step -q pbs_head
sos run script real_step -q pbs
```

The difference is that task from the first command would be executed directly on head node, and the second command would be submitted to PBS.

The convenience of submitting jobs easily to the cluster also makes it easy to submit the jobs incorrectly. For example,

```
sos run script real_step -q pbs_head
```

would easily slow down or even crash the head node.

I am therefore wondering if there can be some safe guard against this kind of incorrect usage. For example, in the `pbs_head` definition, we could define `walltime` or `mem` or something similar to kill the task if they exceed the limit. This is actually not difficult to do because we can code this in our pulse thread and let the pulse thread kill the job when needed.

issue_title: Spurious error message about killed worker when script exits
issue_body:
I sometimes see the following message when a script exits (despite the fact that the script runs to completion without any issues).

```
A worker died or was killed while executing a task.

You can inspect errors by running

    ray.error_info()

If this driver is hanging, start a new one with

    ray.init(redis_address="127.0.0.1:25182")
```

I think the problem is that the script, e.g.,

```python
@ray.remote
def f():
  return 1

ray.get([f.remote() for _ in range(10)])
```

is returning as soon as the object is put in the object store, which may happen before the local scheduler realizes that the task has finished, so the local scheduler will still push an error message about killing a worker that is running a task.

We're already suppressing these error messages that happen during cleanup, but we should also suppress the ones that are triggered by a driver removal.

issue_title: Data migrations doesn't write to ParentalManyToManyField
issue_body:
I ran into an issue writing a data migration for a `ParentalManyToManyField` today where the data migration wouldn't write any data to the tables for that field. The migration looked something like this:

```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
# Generated by Django 1.11.11 on 2018-04-19 14:10
from __future__ import unicode_literals

from django.db import migrations


def forwards(apps, schema_editor):
    """
    Move all data from Topic, Country, and Language to TemporaryTopic,
    TemporaryCountry, and TemporaryLanguage
    """

    TemporaryTopic = apps.get_model('directory', 'TemporaryTopic')
    TemporaryCountry = apps.get_model('directory', 'TemporaryCountry')
    TemporaryLanguage = apps.get_model('directory', 'TemporaryLanguage')
    DirectoryEntry = apps.get_model('directory', 'DirectoryEntry')

    # ...

    # Move DirectoryEntry fks
    for entry in DirectoryEntry.objects.all():
        topic_titles = list(entry.topics.values_list('title', flat=True))
        country_titles = list(entry.countries.values_list('title', flat=True))
        language_titles = list(entry.languages.values_list('title', flat=True))
        entry.temporary_topics.set(
            TemporaryTopic.objects.filter(title__in=topic_titles)
        )
        entry.temporary_countries.set(
            TemporaryCountry.objects.filter(title__in=country_titles)
        )
        entry.temporary_languages.set(
            TemporaryLanguage.objects.filter(title__in=language_titles)
        )
        entry.save()

    # ...

class Migration(migrations.Migration):
    # ...
    operations = [
        migrations.RunPython(forwards),
    ]
```

@gasman suspects the fake model that gets build by django's migration logic doesn't inherit from `ClusterableModel` and is therefore missing some of the logic necessary to make this data migration work.

I ended up using a workaround in which I had a migration convert my `ParentalManyToManyField`s to Django's built-in `ManyToManyField` before the data migration and then another migration afterward to convert them back, which seems to have worked fine.

issue_title: `datacube dataset add/update` should revert failed attempt
issue_body:
### Expected behaviour

When `datacube dataset add` fails to add new dataset to the DB for whatever reason it should leave database in the original state. 

### Actual behaviour

Lineage data might be added to the index even when top level dataset is rejected for whatever reason. Same goes for update.

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

Note this is just from reading the code, not from running an experiment. 

Example: indexing dataset `good_b` derived from `bad`, derived from `good_a` 

`good_b->bad->good_a`

`dataset add` performs a depth first traversal of the lineage tree, so it will successfully add `good_a` first, then will break out when it will see `bad`. So you end up with updated database even though top-level task have failed.

I don't see any transaction setup/reversal in case of an error, only see transaction for updating dataset locations.

issue_title: Shared modules in Python 2.7 cause errors related to restricted mode
issue_body:
When using shared modules, certain functionality is not available because the shared modules are being used in [restricted mode](https://docs.python.org/2/library/restricted.html). For example this java code will generate an exception.

```java
try(Jep jep = new Jep(new JepConfig().addSharedModules("xml.etree.ElementTree"))){
    jep.eval("import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET");
    jep.eval("ET.parse('fakeFile.xml')");
}
```

```
Exception in thread "main" jep.JepException: <type 'exceptions.IOError'>: file() constructor not accessible in restricted mode
	at /awips2/python/lib/python2.7/xml/etree/ElementTree.parse(ElementTree.py:647)
	at /awips2/python/lib/python2.7/xml/etree/ElementTree.parse(ElementTree.py:1182)
```

This issue was originally reported under #62 and also reported in the comments for #55. The two limitations that we have come across so far is that you cannot open files in restricted mode and you cannot unmarshal code objects. I have looked through the cpython source and most of the other checks for restricted mode are complex reflection that I would not expect to come up. The only other code I saw that might cause problems is related to pickling.

The shared modules are not deliberately restricted, it is only a side affect of how they were implemented. The shared modules keep the \_\_builtins\_\_ from the "top interpreter" so when they are used in other jep interpreters the \_\_builtins\_\_ do not match the interpreter and python assumes restricted mode.

The current best solution for this problem is to use python 3, which eliminated restricted mode. Another alternative is to avoid using shared modules which comes with extra limitations on closing jep interpreters.

issue_title: add Graph.fromN3
issue_body:
drewpca, 2008-12-26T22:31:59.000Z 
It would help the creation of our test cases, rdflib's test cases, bug
reports, and tutorials if there was a one-liner to make a graph initialized
from some N3. I propose this API:

from rdflib.Graph import Graph
graph = Graph.fromN3("""
@prefix ...

""")

Comment 1 by lindstr...@gmail.com
In trunk, I think the 'data' parameter to 'Graph.parse' is good enough. Use like::

    from rdflib.graph import Graph
    graph = Graph( format="n3", data="""@prefix ... """)

Can this issue thus be closed? (Sure, the relevant tests should be adapted to use this as well..)


Comment 2 by drewpca
Yes, that's equivalent to what I was looking for. Thanks for the update.

Should we have a label for 'put this in the release notes'?
